purpose.summary: This copybook defines the ERROR-LOG-RECORD structure, which is used for logging errors and informational messages within an application. It includes fields for date, time, application name, program name, location, error level, subsystem, error codes, message, and event key.

paragraphs.purpose: This data structure defines the format for error log records. It contains fields for capturing the date and time of the error (ERR-DATE, ERR-TIME), the application and program where the error occurred (ERR-APPLICATION, ERR-PROGRAM), the location within the program (ERR-LOCATION), the severity level of the error (ERR-LEVEL), and the subsystem where the error originated (ERR-SUBSYSTEM). It also includes fields for two error codes (ERR-CODE-1, ERR-CODE-2), a descriptive error message (ERR-MESSAGE), and an event key for tracking purposes (ERR-EVENT-KEY). The ERR-LEVEL field uses 88-level condition names to define specific error levels such as ERR-LOG, ERR-INFO, ERR-WARNING, and ERR-CRITICAL. Similarly, the ERR-SUBSYSTEM field uses 88-level condition names to define the subsystem where the error occurred, such as ERR-APP, ERR-CICS, ERR-IMS, ERR-DB2, ERR-MQ, and ERR-FILE. This structure serves as a template for creating and storing error information within the system.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'ERROR-LOG-RECORD' is never used by any program

dead_code.name: ERROR-LOG-RECORD
dead_code.reason: Record layout 'ERROR-LOG-RECORD' is never used by any program
